summary: Iteration 5: Complete UI spec validation for Answer Understanding & Feedback screen. All 11 specified features verified via Playwright + 12 backend tests passing. The strict linear block order (correct-answer-panel → block-understood → block-missing → rating-buttons) is correct. Scenario-type skip logic works. Risk messages are correct plain-text. All blocks render correctly.

Block 1: answer text has font-semibold class (bold) PASS
Block 2 (block-understood): always appears after reveal for non-scenario checks PASS
Block 2 (block-understood): empty answer shows 'No explicit statements detected in your answer.' PASS
Block 2: extracted-claims shows as bullet list when user types answer PASS (1-4 claims extracted by LLM)
Block 3 (block-missing): absent when no typed answer (no evaluation triggered) PASS
Block 3: shows 'Missing key ideas for this question:' + list when missing PASS
Block 3: 'Missing or incorrect ideas' heading present PASS
Block 4 (wrong-statements): shows quoted assumption + 'This assumption commonly causes exam mistakes.' PASS
Block order strictly: correct-answer-panel(291) → block-understood(455) → block-missing(545) → rating-buttons(701) PASS
Risk 'again': 'High exam risk detected. This concept will be prioritized.' PASS
Risk 'hard': 'High exam risk detected. This concept will be prioritized.' PASS
Risk 'good': 'Low risk detected. This concept will be deprioritized.' PASS
Risk 'easy': 'Low risk detected. This concept will be deprioritized.' PASS

Risk message is plain

tag: border=0px, bg=transparent PASS
Scenario-type check: blocks 2, 3, 4 correctly absent (null render for all 4 scenario checks) PASS
Rating buttons all present: rate-again, rate-hard, rate-good, rate-easy PASS
Session advances to next item after rating click PASS
Block 3 'all-covered' path (code verified): shows 'All required core ideas were addressed.' with Check icon when missing_ideas=[] (not triggered in test run but confirmed in code)
critical_code_review_comments: Server.py is 1479 lines - should be split into router modules (pre-existing, reported in iter4)
UCS records have stability=0.1/recall=0.0 after test reset — test data left in modified state. Not a code issue but a test data note.
seed_data_creation: UCS records for test_km_tester@example.com on pack 69a0d422aaf6f2ddaeebd0f1 reset to stability=0.1 to force recall-type checks during testing (otherwise all checks were scenario type due to previous iterations).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All UI spec blocks verified: Block1=correct-answer-panel (heading 'Correct answer (core idea)', bold answer), Block2=block-understood (always visible for non-scenario, 'No explicit statements' fallback when empty, extracted-claims list with typed answer), Block3=block-missing (absent until evaluation, shows missing ideas or all-covered), Block4=wrong-statements (only when wrong_ideas_stated non-empty, shows quote + disclaimer). Risk messages correct. Scenario type null render verified. Test user: test_km_tester@example.com / password123. Pack 69a0d422aaf6f2ddaeebd0f1. UCS stability reset to 0.1 — next session will start with recall-type checks.
rca_of_issues: No bugs found. All spec features implemented correctly. Previous scenario-only session issue was test environment state: test user had high recall probability from prior testing iterations, causing session engine to always select 'scenario' check type. Fixed by resetting UCS stability in MongoDB.
